title: ⚡ Bolt: Memoize Dexie live queries to prevent cascading re-renders
body:
💡 What: Wrapped derived arrays and objects created from Dexie's `useLiveQuery` hook in `useMemo` with proper dependency arrays in `Dashboard.tsx`, `ClassroomView.tsx`, `Modules.tsx`, and `LessonView.tsx`.
🎯 Why: `useLiveQuery` triggers a component re-render on any relevant database update. When derived values (like arrays filtered via `.filter()` or objects created via `Object.fromEntries()`) are computed on every render, it destroys React's referential equality. This results in severe performance bottlenecks due to cascading re-renders across the component tree.
📊 Impact: Eliminates unnecessary child component re-renders when the parent component re-renders but the underlying database query results have not changed or when unrelated database updates trigger a re-render.
🔬 Measurement: Verify by profiling the React component tree (e.g., using React DevTools Profiler) while performing background Dexie updates (like syncing tasks or adding notes) on the Dashboard or Classroom views. The component re-renders should be significantly reduced.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [1056629681032519813](https://jules.google.com/task/1056629681032519813) started by @hamido860*

title: 🎨 Palette: Add ARIA labels and keyboard accessibility to Topbar interactive elements
body:
💡 What: Added `aria-label`s and proper keyboard focus indicators to the icon-only buttons (Refresh Connection, Theme switch, Language switch, Settings, Notifications) in the Topbar. Also transformed the profile `div` into an accessible button by adding `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown` handler that correctly delegates 'Enter' and Spacebar keys.

🎯 Why: To ensure the primary navigation controls are fully accessible to screen readers and users who rely on keyboard navigation.

📸 Before/After: Visual changes apply only to focus states when tabbing through the navigation.

♿ Accessibility: Resolves the missing label issue for purely iconographic interactive elements and fixes a common non-semantic clickable `div` pattern by fully implementing WAI-ARIA button criteria.

---

title: 🎨 Palette: Add ARIA labels and focus styles to Topbar buttons
body:
**💡 What:** 
Added descriptive `aria-label`s and visible focus outlines to all five utility icon-only buttons in the Topbar (Refresh Connection, Toggle Theme, Language, Settings, Notifications).

**🎯 Why:**
Icon-only buttons without labels are invisible or read as unhelpful generic text to screen readers. Furthermore, lacking a focus outline makes keyboard navigation nearly impossible as users cannot discern which element is currently active. 

**♿ Accessibility:**
- Added `aria-label` to clearly read out the button's purpose to screen readers.
- Added `focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent focus-visible:ring-offset-1` utility classes to show a clear and brand-consistent outline when navigating via keyboard (Tab key).

---

title: ⚡ Bolt: Memoize useLiveQuery derived data to prevent re-renders
body:
💡 What: Added `useMemo` hooks around derived calculations based on `useLiveQuery` results in `ClassroomView.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `LessonView.tsx`, and `Modules.tsx`.
🎯 Why: `useLiveQuery` from Dexie creates new references for the array or object it returns on any update. If we apply transformations like `Object.fromEntries(dbSettings.map(...))` or `.filter()` without memoizing, it creates a brand new object on every render cycle. This breaks referential equality for child components and triggers cascading, unnecessary re-renders.
📊 Impact: Significantly reduces React re-render overhead across critical views (Dashboard, Modules, Classroom) by caching the derived arrays and configuration maps when the underlying database value hasn't changed.
🔬 Measurement: Run a React Profiler recording while navigating between modules or interacting with the dashboard. Observe the reduced number of component renders.

---

title: 🎨 Palette: Add missing ARIA labels to navigation buttons
body:
### 💡 What
Added missing `aria-label` attributes to various icon-only navigation buttons in the `Topbar.tsx` and `Sidebar.tsx` components. This includes the sidebar collapse toggle, theme toggle, language selector, settings button, notifications bell, and database connection refresh button.

### 🎯 Why
Icon-only buttons rely entirely on their visual representation (the icon) to convey their purpose. While some buttons had a `title` attribute, this is not always reliably announced by all screen readers across all device types. Adding explicit `aria-label`s ensures that users relying on assistive technologies have a clear understanding of the interactive elements available to them, making the application significantly more accessible.

### 📸 Before/After
*(Visuals remain unchanged as this is purely a semantic/accessibility update).*
- **Before:** `<button title="Dark Mode"><Sun /></button>`
- **After:** `<button title="Dark Mode" aria-label="Switch to Dark Mode"><Sun /></button>`

### ♿ Accessibility
This change directly improves keyboard and screen reader accessibility by providing descriptive labels for controls that previously lacked them, ensuring compliance with standard accessibility guidelines.

### 📓 Palette's Journal
A new entry was added to `.jules/palette.md` documenting the importance of adding `aria-label`s to icon-only interactive elements in core navigation components.

---

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [MEDIUM] Fix weak random ID generation
body:
🚨 **Severity:** MEDIUM
💡 **Vulnerability:** Found `Math.random()` used to generate supposedly unique identifiers for study sessions, goals, library items, and unique Supabase real-time channel names. `Math.random()` is not cryptographically secure and leads to predictable, easily guessable identifiers and potential ID collisions.
🎯 **Impact:** Predictable IDs could cause unexpected UI behavior (if React keys collide) or, more importantly, predictable channel names might allow uninvited clients to accidentally (or maliciously) subscribe to specific real-time feeds, exposing internal application state.
🔧 **Fix:** Replaced all instances of `Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)` and `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)` with standard, cryptographically secure `crypto.randomUUID()`.
✅ **Verification:** Verified by confirming the build still completes successfully and reviewing the updated code paths. Run `pnpm build` to verify the code remains structurally sound.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [4607639377235026399](https://jules.google.com/task/4607639377235026399) started by @hamido860*

title: Prefer Supabase-first classroom loading; add AI gating helpers and tests
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure classroom creation remains Supabase-first and AI suggestions are optional and gated by plan status.
- Centralize logic for when to request AI curriculum suggestions and how classroom load actions should behave for free vs Pro users.

### Description
- Added `shouldRequestAiCurriculumSuggestions` to `src/services/classroomLoader.ts` to encapsulate the decision to call AI based on `includeAiSuggestions` and `aiAvailable`.
- Added `getClassroomLoadPlan` and `ClassroomLoadAction` to determine `includeAiSuggestions` for actions like `create_classroom` and `refresh_suggestions` based on `isPro` status.
- Updated `src/pages/Modules.tsx` to import the new helpers, use `getClassroomLoadPlan` when triggering `fetchCurriculum`, and prevent non-Pro users from regenerating AI suggestions by disabling the button for free users.
- Kept existing module mapping and merge behavior and replaced the inline AI-check with a call to `shouldRequestAiCurriculumSuggestions` inside `fetchCurriculum`.

### Testing
- Updated `src/services/__tests__/classroomLoader.test.ts` with unit tests for `shouldRequestAiCurriculumSuggestions` and `getClassroomLoadPlan`, and existing tests for `mapSubjectsToModules`/`mergeModulesWithAiSuggestions` remain present; all tests were executed and succeeded.

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix Hardcoded NVIDIA API Key
body:
## 🚨 Severity: CRITICAL

## 💡 Vulnerability
A hardcoded NVIDIA API fallback key was found in `api/ai-analyst.ts` (`FALLBACK_NVIDIA_KEY = "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"`).

## 🎯 Impact
Hardcoded credentials expose the application to massive security risks. Since the key is baked directly into the application code, anyone with access to the source code (including malicious actors if exposed) could easily scrape and misuse the token. This can result in unauthorized API usage, financial liability (billing implications for external services), and potential data breaches depending on the scope of the token's permissions.

## 🔧 Fix
- Removed the `FALLBACK_NVIDIA_KEY` hardcoded string constant.
- Ensured the `nvidiaKey` initialization securely relies only on the environment variables (`process.env.NVIDIA_API_KEY` or `process.env.VITE_NVIDIA_API_KEY`).
- Maintained the strict failing check to return a `503` if the API key environment variable is unconfigured, preventing unintentional exposure or internal error leaks.
- Documented learning in `.jules/sentinel.md`.

## ✅ Verification
1. Evaluated `api/ai-analyst.ts` to ensure no keys remain hardcoded.
2. Verified the fix by running type-checking (`pnpm lint`) and build processes (`pnpm build`) to ensure there are no compilation issues and functionality continues to function safely.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [15999701295688394101](https://jules.google.com/task/15999701295688394101) started by @hamido860*

title: ⚡ Bolt: Memoize derived data from useLiveQuery
body:
💡 What: Wrapped derived arrays and objects depending on `useLiveQuery` results in `useMemo` hooks.
🎯 Why: `useLiveQuery` triggers a re-render on database updates. Failing to memoize derived data like `.filter()`, `.map()`, or `Object.fromEntries()` creates new object references on every render. This breaks referential equality and causes severe performance bottlenecks through cascading child component re-renders. 
📊 Impact: Prevents unnecessary component re-renders for children dependent on `lessons`, `modules`, `activeModules` and `settingsMap`, significantly reducing CPU overhead during Dexie.js database changes.
🔬 Measurement: Verify by checking React DevTools Profiler while interacting with data stored in the database. Notice fewer cascading re-renders when data points like `modules` or `lessons` are modified.

---

body:

- Converted the non-interactive profile `div` into an accessible button by adding `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown` handler.
- Added explicit `aria-label`s to all topbar icon-only buttons (Database Refresh, Theme, Language, Settings, Notifications).
- Added explicit keyboard focus styling (`focus-visible:ring-2`, etc) to all interactive topbar elements.

🎯 **Why:**
Interactive UI elements must be navigable via keyboards, and icon-only buttons require screen-reader readable text. Without these, visually impaired and keyboard users cannot easily navigate or understand the core application header tools.

📸 **Before/After:**
- *Before:* Users pressing Tab would skip over the profile button or not clearly see which topbar item was focused. Screen readers would just announce "button" or the SVG contents without context.
- *After:* Smooth keyboard navigation across all topbar buttons with a clear visual focus ring, and screen readers will announce explicit actions like "Switch to dark mode" or "Refresh Database Connection".

♿ **Accessibility:**
- Improved WebAIM/WCAG compliance by satisfying focus-visibility and name/role/value guidelines.

title: ⚡ Bolt: memoize useLiveQuery derived objects
body:
💡 What: Wrapped data derived from Dexie `useLiveQuery` results (e.g., `Object.fromEntries(dbSettings.map(...))` and `allModules.filter(...)`) inside `useMemo` hooks in `Dashboard.tsx`, `ClassroomView.tsx`, `LessonView.tsx`, and `Modules.tsx`.
🎯 Why: In React, deriving state inline without memoization causes a new object/array reference to be created on every render. Because `useLiveQuery` intrinsically triggers renders on database updates, this pattern resulted in a massive number of unnecessary cascading re-renders across the component tree.
📊 Impact: Considerably reduces render churn by maintaining referential equality for derived database properties across child components.

title: Make classroom creation Supabase-first and AI-optional
body:
### Motivation
- Prevent CI/UI flows from calling AI during initial classroom creation by making Supabase the primary source of truth for curriculum load.
- Keep AI curriculum generation available but opt-in so failures in AI/embeddings don't block classroom access. 
- Add a small, unit-tested gate to prove the non-AI creation path for free users.

### Description
- Added `CurriculumLoadIntent` and `shouldIncludeAiSuggestions` to `src/services/classroomLoader.ts` to centralize intent-based gating of AI usage and to ensure AI is only included for explicit regenerate flows by checking `aiAvailable` and the intent via `shouldIncludeAiSuggestions`.
- Updated `src/pages/Modules.tsx` to change `fetchCurriculum` to accept an intent (`'create_classroom' | 'regenerate_suggestions'`) and wired the UI so `Create My Classroom` calls `fetchCurriculum('create_classroom')` (no AI) while the `Regenerate` button calls `fetchCurriculum('regenerate_suggestions', true)` (AI-optional).
- Added unit tests in `src/services/__tests__/classroomLoader.test.ts` to assert that `shouldIncludeAiSuggestions` returns `false` for `create_classroom` and only returns `true` for `regenerate_suggestions` when AI is available.
- Preserved existing Supabase-first behavior and did not remove or alter the working PDF/RAG utilities in the repo.

### Testing
- Ran `node --test src/services/__tests__/classroomLoader.test.ts` and all tests passed (4/4).
- Ran `npm run lint` (TypeScript check) which reported pre-existing repo-wide JSX intrinsic typing errors unrelated to this change and not introduced by these edits.

------

title: ⚡ Bolt: Add useMemo to derived Dexie queries
body:
💡 **What**: Added `useMemo` to variables derived from `useLiveQuery` results (like mapping `dbSettings` to `settingsMap`, or filtering `allModules` and `allLessons`) across `Dashboard.tsx`, `ClassroomView.tsx`, `LessonView.tsx`, and `Modules.tsx`.
🎯 **Why**: Dexie's `useLiveQuery` frequently triggers re-renders on database changes. When query results are mapped into new objects or filtered arrays on every render, it creates new memory references. This defeats React's reference-equality checks and causes massive cascading re-renders in child components.
📊 **Impact**: Prevents redundant object creation and significantly reduces unnecessary re-renders in major dashboard and view components, leading to a smoother UX.
🔬 **Measurement**: Use React DevTools Profiler to record component renders while interacting with settings or modules. The number of child component renders should decrease significantly when no underlying data changed.

body:
🚨 Severity: CRITICAL

🎯 Impact: Attackers could extract the key and run up large NVIDIA API bills, or abuse the `.env` default configuration to perform unauthorized requests against the database.
🔧 Fix: Removed `FALLBACK_NVIDIA_KEY` from `api/ai-analyst.ts`, removed `.env` from tracking, and added `.env` to `.gitignore`.

title: Make classroom loading Supabase‑first and make AI suggestions optional (#40)
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure classroom creation/loading does not depend on AI so free users and offline flows work when AI or embedding/RAG services fail. 
- Move AI curriculum generation to an opt‑in suggestion path so failures do not block access to classrooms.
- Improve testability and prevent runtime crashes when importing Supabase client in non‑Vite (node test) contexts.

### Description
- Added a Supabase‑first classroom loader service in `src/services/classroomService.ts` implementing `loadModulesFromSupabase` and `loadClassroomModulesSupabaseFirst` to fetch grade → grade_subjects → subjects and optionally merge non‑blocking AI suggestions. 
- Updated the Modules UI (`src/pages/Modules.tsx`) so the primary Create flow calls the Supabase‑first loader and no longer requires an AI provider; AI is exposed as a separate "AI Suggestions" action. 
- Made the Supabase client import resilient to non‑Vite runtimes by guarding `import.meta.env` access in `src/db/supabase.ts`. 
- Added unit tests `src/services/__tests__/classroomService.test.ts` that mock a Supabase‑like client and verify Supabase‑first behavior and that AI suggestion failures do not block access. 
- Introduced dependency injection for Supabase client in `classroomService` to simplify testing and to ensure AI is optional.

### Testing
- Ran the new node tests: `node --import tsx --test src/services/__tests__/classroomService.test.ts` and all tests passed (3/3). 
- Attempted project lint (`npm run lint` / `tsc --noEmit`) and observed pre‑existing TypeScript/JSX typing errors elsewhere in the repo; lint did not pass but errors are outside this change set. 

Remaining risks
- Grade name matching relies on `ilike` and may miss curricula if naming variants/aliases differ; further aliasing or mapping may be needed. 
- Subject category inference is heuristic and may misclassify localized subject names. 
- AI suggestion de‑duplication is by name only and may still produce near‑duplicates; more sophisticated matching could be added later. 
- There remain unrelated TypeScript/JSX typing issues in the repo that keep the global lint/typecheck from passing; they are outside the scope of this PR.

------

title: Rebrand AI features to Guided/Content Pipeline, add Supabase-first classroom loading and classroomService utilities with tests
body:
### Motivation
- Update user-facing copy to downplay the term "AI" and emphasize guided learning, quality checks, and curriculum alignment.
- Make classroom loading independent from the presence of an AI provider by favoring Supabase-first loading and clearer UX when guided tools are unavailable.
- Provide small utilities for building Supabase lesson filters and modules from lesson rows to centralize classroom construction logic.

### Description
- Renamed and reworded many UI labels, placeholders and titles across components (for example `AI Tutor` → `Guided Tutor`, `AI Generated` → `Lesson draft`, `Thinking...` → `Preparing help...`, and related translations in `LanguageContext`).
- Introduced `src/constants/uiCopy.ts` to centralize preferred user-facing copy rules and mappings.
- Added new service `src/services/classroomService.ts` exposing `buildSupabaseLessonFilters` and `buildModulesFromLessons` to decouple Supabase classroom loading logic from AI features.
- Wired Supabase-first classroom loading and a `loadClassroomsFromSupabase` action into `Modules` and updated `ClassroomView` to use settings from local Dexie DB, plus various UX text updates to reflect guided workflows.
- Miscellaneous UI and accessibility tweaks including `index.css` font stack adjustments and multiple copy updates in admin/command center, assistant, workspace tools, lesson pages, and settings.
- Added unit tests `src/services/__tests__/classroomService.test.ts` validating the new `classroomService` utilities.

### Testing
- Ran unit tests with `npm test` and the new `classroomService` tests (`buildSupabaseLessonFilters` and `buildModulesFromLessons`) passed.
- Verified no test failures in the existing test suite after the changes.

title: Fix #40: Make classroom loading Supabase-first and AI optional
body:
### Motivation
- Ensure classroom creation and loading never require AI so free users and offline flows are not blocked by AI/embeddings/RAG failures.
- Move AI curriculum generation from a blocking path into an optional suggestion/regeneration path to reduce outage risk.
- Prefer authoritative Supabase curriculum as the source-of-truth and only augment it with AI suggestions when available.

### Description
- Added `src/services/classroomLoader.ts` with `mapSubjectsToModules` to convert Supabase `subjects` rows into local `Module` entries and `mergeModulesWithAiSuggestions` to non-destructively combine optional AI suggestions.
- Updated `src/pages/Modules.tsx` so `Create My Classroom` loads modules from Supabase first (via `supabase.from('subjects')`) and writes to IndexedDB without requiring AI; the regenerate flow still calls `generateCurriculum` but only as an optional enhancement.
- Adjusted UI copy and icons to reflect "Supabase-first, AI-optional" behavior and ensured AI failures are caught and logged without preventing classroom access.
- Added unit tests at `src/services/__tests__/classroomLoader.test.ts` validating `mapSubjectsToModules` preserves UUID ids and `mergeModulesWithAiSuggestions` preserves Supabase entries while appending unique AI suggestions.
- Notes and remaining risks: the Supabase query currently uses `select('*').limit(500)` and may need server-side filtering/pagination for large datasets, and repo-wide TypeScript/JSX typing errors remain outside the scope of this change.

### Testing
- Ran the new unit tests with `node --import tsx --test src/services/__tests__/classroomLoader.test.ts` and the tests passed (2/2). 
- Ran `npm run lint` to check project types and the lint step failed due to pre-existing TypeScript/JSX typing issues unrelated to the classroom-loading changes.

------

title: Fix #40: Make classroom creation Supabase-first and AI optional
body:
### Motivation
- Prevent AI availability from blocking students creating or loading classrooms by prioritizing Supabase curriculum data.
- Ensure the “Create My Classroom” flow does not call AI and that AI curriculum generation is only optional suggestions.
- Make classroom loading resilient for free users and when AI / RAG / embeddings / lesson generation fail.

### Description
- Updated `src/pages/Modules.tsx` so the empty-state CTA (`Create My Classroom`) loads the classroom from Supabase first and separated the optional `Get AI Suggestions` action, removing AI as a hard dependency for classroom creation. 
- Added `src/services/classroomCatalogService.ts` which queries Supabase curriculum tables (`grades`, `grade_subjects`, `subjects`, optional `bac_track_subjects`) and writes modules to local IndexedDB. 
- Added `src/services/classroomCatalogCore.ts` which orchestrates a Supabase-first load and treats AI suggestions as optional and failure-tolerant; AI failures are logged and do not block the flow. 
- Added unit tests `src/services/__tests__/classroomCatalogService.test.ts` that use the core orchestrator with injected deps to assert Supabase-first behavior and that optional AI failures do not prevent loading. 

Changed files:
- src/pages/Modules.tsx
- src/services/classroomCatalogService.ts (new)
- src/services/classroomCatalogCore.ts (new)
- src/services/__tests__/classroomCatalogService.test.ts (new)

### Testing
- Ran the new node-style tests: `node --import tsx --test src/services/__tests__/classroomCatalogService.test.ts` and the two tests passed, verifying Supabase-first loading and that optional AI failures are tolerated. (✅)
- Ran `npm run lint` which fails due to pre-existing TypeScript/JSX typing errors elsewhere in the repo and is unrelated to this change; the lint failure should be addressed separately in CI if needed. (⚠️)

Remaining risks
- Supabase data availability or mismatched grade/track labels can result in an empty catalog; the code will not block but may leave the classroom empty.
- The Supabase queries assume certain schema/label normalization (`ilike('name', grade)`), which may need adjustment for robust international grade naming.
- UI wording and UX coupling between loading and AI suggestion flows could be refined in follow-ups for clearer separation.
- Repo-wide TypeScript/JSX type errors currently cause `npm run lint` to fail; they are pre-existing and do not affect the runtime behavior introduced by this PR.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69f079052588832eb3dfdc2c679b5f39)

title: Fix NVIDIA API CORS proxy issue and Supabase unmount websocket errors
body:
This PR addresses two critical issues reported:

1.  **NVIDIA API CORS Error**: The browser client previously made direct calls to the NVIDIA API endpoint (`https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions`) inside `callNvidiaAPI` in `src/services/geminiService.ts`. Since the NVIDIA API does not return permissive CORS headers, these calls were blocked by the browser. To resolve this, a new proxy endpoint was created at `api/nvidia-proxy.ts` using Vercel's Serverless functions. `callNvidiaAPI` was then updated to conditionally route requests through `/api/nvidia-proxy` when executing in the browser (`typeof window !== 'undefined'`).
2.  **Supabase WebSocket Interruption Error**: The `createDummyClient()` function in `src/db/supabase.ts`, used when Supabase credentials are not valid or offline, lacked the `removeChannel` method. This caused unmount errors across the application (e.g. `useAppSettings`) when React components attempted to cleanup their active Supabase realtime channel subscriptions (`supabase.removeChannel(channel)`), which presented as websocket interruption alerts. The `removeChannel` stub was added to the dummy client to prevent these crashes.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [713513340720929759](https://jules.google.com/task/713513340720929759) started by @hamido860*

title: ⚡ Bolt: Memoize Dexie useLiveQuery derived data
body:
💡 What: Wrapped derived data calculations (`Object.fromEntries`, `.filter`) relying on `useLiveQuery` outputs in `useMemo`.
🎯 Why: `useLiveQuery` triggers a re-render on any database update. If derived data is constructed inline without memoization, it creates new object references on every render cycle, leading to severe performance bottlenecks via cascading re-renders of child components.
📊 Impact: Significantly reduces unnecessary React component re-renders and stabilizes object references for contexts like `settingsMap` and `activeModules` across the app (Dashboard, ClassroomView, LessonView, Modules).
🔬 Measurement: Verify with React DevTools Profiler to observe reduced re-render cycles when database settings or modules are updated.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [3222218612148441142](https://jules.google.com/task/3222218612148441142) started by @hamido860*

title: 🎨 Palette: Improve Topbar accessibility with ARIA labels and focus styles
body:
💡 What: Added `aria-label`s and consistent keyboard focus styling (`focus-visible:ring`) to all icon-only buttons (Refresh, Theme, Language, Settings, Notifications) and the Search input in the Topbar component. Created `.jules/palette.md` to journal this accessibility pattern.
🎯 Why: Icon-only buttons without text labels are completely invisible or unhelpful to screen readers. Adding ARIA labels provides the necessary context. Additionally, explicit `focus-visible` styles ensure users navigating via keyboard can clearly see which interactive element currently has focus, which is a critical WCAG requirement.
📸 Before/After: (See Playwright UI verification screenshot)
♿ Accessibility:
- Improves screen reader support by adding descriptive names to interactive elements.
- Enhances keyboard navigation by introducing a high-contrast focus ring that adheres to the established Tailwind pattern.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [14747110258534138572](https://jules.google.com/task/14747110258534138572) started by @hamido860*

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix hardcoded API key and exposed secrets
body:
🚨 **Severity:** CRITICAL
💡 **Vulnerability:** A hardcoded NVIDIA API key was left as a fallback in the `api/ai-analyst.ts` serverless function. Additionally, the `.env` file (containing the key and sensitive Supabase credentials) was checked into source control due to a missing `.gitignore` entry.
🎯 **Impact:** If exploited or accessed publicly, the hardcoded and committed keys could be used to incur unauthorized API billing charges or manipulate backend data.
🔧 **Fix:** Removed the hardcoded fallback value from the code, updated the function to use `process.env` exclusively, added `.env` to `.gitignore`, and completely untracked `.env` from Git. Also logged a new entry in `.jules/sentinel.md` outlining the incident.
✅ **Verification:** Verified that `.env` is ignored via `git status`, confirmed code structure builds successfully via `pnpm build`, and ensured the fallback was successfully removed via file reads. Note: Since `.env` was committed to history previously, the actual NVIDIA key should still be considered compromised and rotated at the provider level.

---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [5554527809047709480](https://jules.google.com/task/5554527809047709480) started by @hamido860*

title: Fix 405 error on Admin dashboard operations
body:
The `Admin.tsx` page was trying to execute API requests to imaginary endpoints (like `/api/admin/retry-job`), resulting in 405 empty response errors. I replaced the `adminPost` function with direct Supabase client queries for updating, deleting, and inserting data (which handles Row Level Security natively).

title: Remove AI dependency for core Classroom UX and modules load
body:
- Removed automatic AI generation and fetch from `ClassroomView.tsx`, fetching exclusively from Supabase logic (`grade_subjects` → `topics` → `lessons`).
- Added graceful fallbacks such as `Lessons are being prepared` when content is unavailable.
- Unblocked module selection for `Create My Classroom` by completely bypassing `checkAIProvider()`.
- Re-architected `fetchCurriculum` inside `Modules.tsx` to read directly from database mappings.
- Adjusted onboarding to correctly retrieve and save entity UUIDs directly from Supabase DB to `localStorage` and `IndexedDB`.
- Appended centralized tracking logs for AI invocations via `[AI_CALL]` across `geminiService.ts`.
- Included basic sanity checks within `ClassroomView.test.tsx` verifying decoupling logic.
